exercises.name: Isometric table top w/ add
exercises.description: No block ? Use a towel or ball or balloon!

exercises.name: Jog On The Spot
exercises.alternateName: Run on the spot, High knees
exercises.description: Jog in place, lifting your knees slightly and maintaining a quick pace to elevate your heart rate.

exercises.name: Jump Squat to Lateral Shuffle
exercises.description:
Start in an athletic stance with your feet shoulder-width apart. Lower into a squat, then explosively jump upward, fully extending your hips, knees, and ankles. 
Land softly back into a squat position, maintaining control and balance. 

Immediately perform a quick lateral shuffle to one side for a few steps, then repeat the jump squat and shuffle in the opposite direction. Continue alternating sides in a smooth, continuous motion.

exercises.name: Jump Squat to Reverse Lunge
exercises.alternateName: Jump Squat to Alternating Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: Squat then jump explosively, then step back into a reverse lunge.

exercises.name: Jumping Jack
exercises.description:
1) Keep your arms extended. 

2) Jump from your legs.

exercises.name: Jumping Jack Press
exercises.description:
1) When performing a jumping jack, do a shoulder press. 

2) When you lock out overhead, ensure your arm is in line with your ear. 

3) Repeat the desired number of reps.

exercises.name: Jumping Pull Up
exercises.description:
1. Jump upwards to grab a pull up bar at about shoulder-width. 

3. Using your momentum, immediately pull yourself upwards until your chin is above the bar. Lower yourself to the floor and repeat.

exercises.name: Jumping Rope
exercises.description:
1) Hold an end of the rope in each hand. Position the rope behind you on the ground. 

2) Raise your arms up and turn the rope over your head bringing it down in front of you.

exercises.name: Jumping Split Squat
exercises.description: From a split stance, perform small explosive jumps while maintaining your position, landing softly and repeating the movement continuously.

exercises.name: KAS Hip thruster
exercises.description: KAS Hip thruster

exercises.name: KB SL RDL
exercises.description: Weight stays on front leg. Keep core TIGHT so that lower back does not ache. Leg is back is on toe, using it for balance not weight bearing.

exercises.name: Kettlebell (KB)/Dumbbell (DB) Deadlift
exercises.description: Kettlebell (KB)/Dumbbell (DB) Deadlift

exercises.name: Kettlebell 21
exercises.description:
1. Stand upright and grab kettlebells with both hands. Place your hands shoulders-width apart and allow your arms to hang toward the floor.

2. Tuck your elbows tight to the sides of your body.

3. Curl upward until you make a 90-degree angle at your elbows. Relax your arms back to full extension and repeat six more curls reaching the 90-degree angle at your elbow.

4. Now, from the 90-degree at your elbow position, curl the weight up until the kettlebells are one to two inches away from your shoulder.

5. Lower the weight back to the 90-degree elbow position and repeat six more times.

6. Now, allow your arms to return to full extension.

7. This time, curl your arms from full extension all the way to full extension. Keep curling until the bar is about one to two inches away from your shoulder.

8. Repeat six more curls through this full range of motion to complete a total of 21 reps.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Around The World
exercises.description: Hold a kettlebell in both hands, and circle it around your head while maintaining a firm core and upright posture.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Bent Over Row
exercises.description:
1) Bend your knees slightly and push your butt out as much as possible, holding a kettlebell in each hand, place them in front of the body away from your knees.

2) Keeping back straight, pull one kettlebell off of the floor while holding on to the other kettlebell.

3) Lower the kettlebell in the working arm and repeat with your other arm.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Clean
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, you want to ensure a safe back. Keep your back set in a slightly arched position. 

2) As you pull the Kettlebell up, you want to drive from the legs and follow through with your arms scooping under the Kettlebell. 

3) Place a kettlebell between your feet. As you bend down to grab the kettlebell, push your butt back and keep your eyes looking forward.

4) Clean the kettlebell to your shoulders by extending through the legs and hips as you raise the kettlebell towards your shoulder. The wrist should rotate as you do so.

5) Return to starting position and repeat with the other arm.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Floor Press
exercises.alternateName: Kettlebell Alternating Chest Press on Floor
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, safely roll the kettlebells up towards your shoulder before pressing them above your body.

2. Lie on the floor holding two kettlebells with both hands.

3. Extend both arms, so that the kettlebells are being held above your chest. 

4. Lower one kettlebell, bringing it close your chest. Raise the kettlebell and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Forward Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in each hand at your sides. Step forward into a lunge with one leg, then alternate legs. 

This works quads, glutes, and enhances coordination.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Halo
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, ensure that your abdominals are set to protect your lower back while the kettlebell is going around your head. 

2) Repeat. 

3) Then back the other way.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Halo with Chest Press
exercises.description:
Hold a single kettlebell with both hands at chest level. Move the kettlebell in a halo motion around your head, keeping your core tight and elbows slightly bent. 

After completing the halo, immediately perform a chest press by pushing the kettlebell straight out from your chest. 

Alternate which direction you halo around your head on the next rep, maintaining a stable stance and strong core throughout.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Lateral Lunge and Reach
exercises.description:
Lower into a lateral lunge by bending the knee of the leg you’re lunging toward while keeping the opposite leg straight. 

As you descend, reach the kettlebell toward the foot of the lunging leg. Push through the bent leg to return to the starting position, then alternate sides, switching the kettlebell to the opposite hand as you lunge on the other side. 

Repeat continuously.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding a kettlebell in each hand in the top position at your shoulders.

2.Press one while holding the other kettlebell stationary.

3.Lower the pressed kettlebell and immediately press the kettlebell with your other arm.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in each hand at your sides. Step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then alternate legs. 

This targets legs, glutes, and improves balance.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Rotational Press
exercises.alternateName: KB twist with single arm shoulder press
exercises.description:
Start with a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height. 

Press one kettlebell overhead while rotating your torso towards the opposite side. 

Lower and repeat on the other side. This works the shoulders, core, and promotes rotational strength.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Alternating Stationary Cossack Squat
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell at chest level. Shift your weight onto one leg and bend that knee, lowering your hips into a deep side squat while keeping the opposite leg straight and your chest upright. 

Push through the bent leg to return to the center, then shift to the other leg, continuing to alternate sides with each rep. Keep the kettlebell close to your chest throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Anterior Loaded Good Morning
exercises.description:
Stand with a kettlebell in front of you, holding it with both hands. 

Hinge at your hips (not your back) and lower your torso, keeping your chest lifted and knees slightly bent. 

This strengthens your hamstrings, glutes, and lower back.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Arm Bar
exercises.description:
Lie on your back and hold a kettlebell in one hand with your arm fully extended. 

Slowly rotate your body while keeping the kettlebell overhead. 

This works shoulders, stability, and mobility.
